name: List
author: Martin Macak
description: Dynamic data table for the array data source — the first of its kind in HexaOS. Bind any number of datapoints into one input; each becomes a reorderable row. Per row you set the icon, colour, label and unit override, and choose what the value column shows: a read-out, a live Control for a writable point, or a rolling Sparkline. Control mode is auto-detected from the datapoint (bool → switch, enum → dropdown, numeric → slider or number, text → input) or chosen explicitly — including an icon-toggle that flips between two status colours — and writes changes back to the device. Global options cover density, dividers, zebra, icon/name/value sizes and colours, value weight, decimals, units, sparkline points/min/max/colour and an optional header. No auto or random icons or colours — every visual aspect is user-configured.
